description: Baseline trace recorded: list-load LCP, render delay, first-load JS, and per-row prefetch count captured in SCRATCHPAD
Baseline next-build first-load JS for /msp/tickets recorded; 7 dialogs + ClientDetails confirmed present in baseline chunk graph
On list load, network capture shows zero GET /msp/tickets/<id>?_rsc=... prefetch requests
Clicking the ticket-number link opens the ticket (onTicketClick fired) and does not full-navigate
Clicking the title link opens the ticket (onTicketClick fired)
Cmd/Ctrl+click and middle-click on a row open /msp/tickets/[id] in a new tab (href preserved)
Unit/contract test: ticket-columns renders Links with prefetch={false}
Lightweight details-tab component renders client details fields identically to the previous quickView tab (snapshot/visual)
Static-import assertion: ClientQuickView module graph does NOT include BillingConfiguration, InteractionsFeed, ClientContactsList, ClientNotesPanel, or the Hudu tab modules
ClientQuickView opens in a drawer and shows the same content as before across a sample call site (tickets list)
Full-page ClientDetails still renders all tabs correctly after the details-tab extraction (no regression)
Tickets-list drawer quick view (MspTicketsPageClient) renders ClientQuickView and matches prior appearance
Ticket-detail client drawer renders ClientQuickView correctly
MspClientDrawerProvider renders ClientQuickView correctly
Billing dashboard client drawer renders ClientQuickView correctly
Projects integration client quick view renders ClientQuickView correctly
Clients page quick view renders ClientQuickView correctly
Interactions client quick view renders ClientQuickView correctly
Settings user-list client quick view renders ClientQuickView correctly
Contact-context <ClientDetails> usages reviewed; migrated ones render correctly, intentionally-full ones documented
Build/chunk assertion: full ClientDetails no longer in the tickets-list route first-load graph
Quick view still supports its existing actions/links (e.g. open full client page) from the lighter component
tickets/layout.tsx renders children + @modal slot without altering the default list appearance
@modal/default.tsx renders null so the list shows no modal on normal load
Tickets context provides current filters matching the active list filters
Tickets context provides selectedTicketIds matching the rows checked in the list
Selecting/deselecting rows updates the shared context (and the action bar) with no behavior change vs baseline
Import prototype: opening route shows modal overlay with list still mounted underneath
Import prototype: closing modal (Esc/backdrop/close) returns to list without full reload; back button closes modal
Import prototype: hard refresh on the import URL renders the full-page fallback without crashing
Import modal performs an import correctly and the list refreshes to reflect imported tickets
After import extraction, TicketingDashboard no longer imports TicketImportDialog (grep/build assertion)
Export modal opens as overlay and exports respecting the current filters (filtered set only)
Export respects a non-default filter combination (e.g. board + status + tag) end-to-end
After export extraction, TicketingDashboard no longer imports TicketExportDialog
Bulk Assign modal acts on exactly the selected ticket ids from context
Bulk Assign respects 'select all matching filter' selections (filtered superset), not just visible page
Bulk Add Tags modal applies tags to the selected set and refreshes the list
Bulk Set Due Date modal updates due dates for the selected set
Bulk Change Status modal updates statuses for the selected set (board-scoped statuses load correctly)
Bulk Change Priority modal updates priorities for the selected set
Each bulk modal surfaces per-ticket errors the same way as the old in-list dialogs
After bulk extraction, TicketingDashboard imports none of the 5 bulk dialogs
Selection clears appropriately after a successful bulk action (matches prior behavior)
DoD-2: next build shows reduced first-load JS for /msp/tickets vs baseline; 7 dialogs absent from list chunk graph
DoD-1: repeat perf trace shows reduced render delay and LCP vs 2774ms/3048ms baseline; numbers recorded
DoD-6: repo grep confirms no next/dynamic, React.lazy, or import() added by this work
DoD-5/C1: manual + visual pass confirms dialogs still appear as modal overlays and client drawer is visually unchanged
Regression: existing tickets-list e2e/integration suites pass (filters, pagination, sorting, row selection, quick add)
Multi-tenant: bulk/import/export/client-fetch via new routes remain tenant-scoped (no cross-tenant leakage)
